title: Schedule a pod on a tainted GPU node
scenario: Node `sim-node-2` is reserved for GPU workloads and has the taint `dedicated=gpu:NoSchedule`. Your `gpu-job` pod must run on that node but it keeps getting rejected because it has no toleration. Add the required toleration and a nodeSelector so the pod is placed correctly.
starterYaml:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: gpu-job
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: gpu-job
spec:
  restartPolicy: Never
  nodeSelector:
    kubernetes.io/hostname: sim-node-2
  containers:
  - name: gpu-job
    image: alpine:3.18
    command: ["/bin/sh", "-c", "echo GPU workload complete; sleep 30"]
    resources:
      requests:
        cpu: "100m"
        memory: "64Mi"

objectives.description: Pod has a toleration for key `dedicated`, value `gpu`, effect `NoSchedule`

hints.text: Check the pod events: `kubectl describe pod gpu-job`. You will see `0/2 nodes are available: 1 node(s) had untolerated taint {dedicated: gpu}`.
Add a `tolerations` section to the pod spec. You need to tolerate key `dedicated`, value `gpu`, operator `Equal`, effect `NoSchedule`.

Here is the exact stanza to add under `spec`:
  tolerations:
  - key: dedicated
    operator: Equal
    value: gpu
    effect: NoSchedule

solution:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: gpu-job
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: gpu-job
spec:
  restartPolicy: Never
  nodeSelector:
    kubernetes.io/hostname: sim-node-2
  tolerations:
  - key: dedicated
    operator: Equal
    value: gpu
    effect: NoSchedule
  containers:
  - name: gpu-job
    image: alpine:3.18
    command: ["/bin/sh", "-c", "echo GPU workload complete; sleep 30"]
    resources:
      requests:
        cpu: "100m"
        memory: "64Mi"
